Lebanese president hopes to generate pressure on Israel during US trip
articles.summary: Lebanese president hopes to generate pressure on Israel during US trip  Lebanese President Joseph Aoun will make his first trip to the White House this week to present a plan to US president Donald Trump on how to disarm Hezbollah and secure Israel's withdrawal from Lebanon. Aoun is the first Lebanese head of state in nearly 20 years to visit the White House, where he will meet Trump face-to-face for the first time. Tuesday's meeting comes at a crucial moment for Lebanon: Israeli troops are occupying a swathe of the country's south, hundreds of thousands of Lebanese remain displaced follo

articles.title: Israeli military closes area around Gaza ahead of settler march, report says
articles.summary: Israeli military closes area around Gaza ahead of settler march, report says Israeli military has declared the entire Gaza periphery region a closed military zone, ahead of a march planned by a right-wing group that seeks to reestablish Israeli settlements inside the territory, Israeli media reported. The March of Thousands, organised by the Nahala settler group, was expected to start from the Yad Mordechai area at 5 pm local time, the Times of Israel reported. The organisers had the goal of “returning home after 21 years,” a reference to the removal of Israeli settlements inside Gaza.

Emergency services on alert in Iran's earthquake-hit Khuzestan
articles.summary: Emergency services on alert in Iran's earthquake-hit Khuzestan Iranian authorities have put emergency services on alert in Khuzestan province, which was hit by a magnitude 5 earthquake this morning. Khuzestan governor, Seyed Mohammad Reza Mowlaizadeh, said he has issued the “necessary instructions for the full mobilisation of rescue and operational forces”, Al Jazeera reported. Mowlaizadeh said no casualties or significant financial damage had yet been reported in the region, and official teams were assessing the situation.

articles.title: Palestinians bid farewell to teenage footballer shot during settler raid
articles.summary: Palestinians bid farewell to teenage footballer shot during settler raid Palestinians have bid farewell to a 17-year-old footballer, Fadi Al-Naasan, who died on Saturday of bullet wounds sustained during an Israeli settler attack on the village of Al-Mughayyir in the occupied West Bank a week ago. Al-Naasan was shot in the thigh with live ammunition on 11 July when Israeli forces and settlers attacked the home of Mohammed Hamed Abu Aliya in Al-Mughayyir, according to media reports.  The farewell of the 17-year-old youth, Fadi Hamdallah Al-Naasan, from the village of Al-Mughayyir, who

articles.summary: Iranian commander warns of 'devastating response' to aggression A top Iranian military commander, Major-General Ali Abdullahi, warned that “any aggression or barbarism will be met with a decisive and devastating response” from the country’s armed forces, Al Jazeera reported. Abdullahi, the commander of Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, pledged to strengthen unity between the armed forces, the Iranian public and officials and pledged to “impose heavier costs” on the US than in the previous wars. “The enemy, following successive defeats in the military conflict, has taken comfort in

articles.title: Centcom: IRGC forces behind Jordan attack targeted in eighth round of strikes
articles.summary: Centcom: IRGC forces behind Jordan attack targeted in eighth round of strikes The US military said it carried out an eighth consecutive night of strikes on Iran late on Saturday, targeting defence facilities and Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces who launched the attacks on US service members in Jordan on Friday. In a statement, Centcom said coastal surveillance and air defence facilities, maritime capabilities, and missile and drone storage sites were targeted during the strikes that were "intended to further degrade Iran's military capabilities". articles.title: Iranian drones strike two US bases in Kuwait, report says
articles.summary: Iranian drones strike two US bases in Kuwait, report says Kuwait’s Ali Al-Salem base and Camp Udairi came under attack of Iranian army’s kamikaze drones during the 16th phase of Operation Sa'eqeh (Thunderbolt), Iranian media reported. IRNA said Camp Udairi is one of the major US bases in the region, located about 104 kilometres from Iran’s borders, serving as a key support and reorganization centre for American forces. articles.title: Report: Aipac stops taking donations for Democrats who backed Israel aid cut
articles.summary: Report: Aipac stops taking donations for Democrats who backed Israel aid cut The American Israel Public Affairs Committee (Aipac), a leading pro-Israel lobbying group in the United States, has stopped accepting campaign contributions for 15 House Democrats it had previously endorsed, after they voted this week for an amendment to cut off all US military aid to Israel, media reports said. The amendment, introduced by Kentucky Republican Thomas Massie as part of a State Department spending bill, was defeated 314 to 104, but drew support from 103 House Democrats, nearly half the caucus. articles.title: Family of five, including three children, killed in Israeli strike on Gaza
articles.summary: Family of five, including three children, killed in Israeli strike on Gaza A family of five -- three children and their parents -- was among the 11 Palestinians killed in Israeli strikes in Gaza on Saturday, according to the territory’s civil defence and hospitals. A ceasefire remains in place between Israel and Hamas, but it has not halted the violence in Gaza, and efforts to secure a lasting agreement to end the war have stalled. The family was killed when an Israeli strike hit a residential apartment in the northwest of Gaza City, according to the territory's civil defence agency. "The

articles.title: US strike hits a location in Qeshm Island, Iranian media says
articles.summary: US strike hits a location in Qeshm Island, Iranian media says The office of the governor of Iran's Hormozgan province said that an American strike hit a location in Qeshm at around 3.40 am local time on Sunday, according to the state news agency IRNA. Residents had reported hearing multiple blasts in Bandar Abbas and Qeshm Island, but no impact or strike in Bandar Abbas has been confirmed yet, IRNA reported. articles.title: Emergency water delivery begins for 10,000 Iranians, official says
articles.summary: Emergency water delivery begins for 10,000 Iranians, official says Emergency delivery of drinking water was underway in about 20 Iranian villages, with a population of around 10,000 people, after a US strike on a desalination plant in the port city of Jask in Hormozgan province cut off the supply on Friday night. Citing the governor of Jask, Mohmmad Jamaleddini, IRNA reported that water was being supplied from alternative sources and mobile tankers were being deployed. articles.title: Reports: Blasts heard in Iranian port cities of Bandar Abbas, Bandar Lengeh
articles.summary: Reports: Blasts heard in Iranian port cities of Bandar Abbas, Bandar Lengeh Explosion sounds were heard in the strategically important southern Iranian port cities of Bandar Abbas and Bandar Lengeh, according to Iran’s semi-official Tasnim news agency. Tasnim reported a US strike near Haji Abad in Bandar Abbas, which caused no civilian casualties or damage to residential and commercial infrastructure. articles.title: Israeli settlers storm Palestinian homes in Hebron, steal sheep
articles.summary: Israeli settlers storm Palestinian homes in Hebron, steal sheep Israeli settlers attacked Palestinian homes in the Wadi al-Rakhim area south of Yatta, near Hebron, on Saturday evening, stealing three sheep, according to the Palestinian news agency Wafa. Local anti-settlement activist Osama Makhamreh said the armed settlers were accompanied by Israeli forces, who fired live ammunition during the raid. No injuries were reported, but a child, Yaseen Mohammad Shnaran, was hospitalised after suffering severe shock and convulsions. In a separate incident, armed settlers, also reportedly under I

articles.title: Iran closes two border crossings with Kurdish region of Iraq
articles.summary: Iran closes two border crossings with Kurdish region of Iraq Iran has closed the Marivan and Hawraman border crossings with Iraq and urged residents to stay away from the surrounding frontier areas in Kurdistan province, Kurdish-language broadcaster Aryen TV reported.  Border guards warned citizens not to approach the border strip in Dehreh-ki, Dezli and other frontier areas, advising against any movement or activity there, according to the report. articles.title: Trump says deaths of US service members ‘very sad’
articles.summary: Trump says deaths of US service members ‘very sad’ US President Donald Trump said deaths of two US service members in Iranian attacks is “a very sad thing,” as he reacted to the announcement. “We hate to see it happen. It’s in service to our country,” Trump told NewsNation. He  also said that the US would “never” allow Iran to obtain a nuclear weapon. US President Donald Trump speaks to reporters aboard the new Air Force One during a flight from the United Kingdom to Joint Base Andrews, 8 July 2026 (Win McNamee Getty Images via AFP)
